results.prompt:
I'm getting these weird super thin report sections sometimes, like the model
clearly started the section but then it kind of gives up and we get a shallow
stub instead of a real writeup. I first thought the model was just being lazy,
but it feels too consistent for that. I've tried tweaking prompts and it still
happens. Can you trace what in this codebase could cause that behavior?
results.final_response:
The thin sections are much more likely coming from the report pipeline than from the model “being lazy”.

Most likely cause: once a section has hit the minimum 3 tool calls, the code accepts any non-tool, non-`Final Answer:` response as the finished section, with no quality check. So if the model starts drafting, trails off, or emits a half-formed stub, that partial text is immediately saved as final. See [`report_agent.py:1470`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py) through [`report_agent.py:1500`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py). The forced-finalization path has the same problem: after max iterations it sends one last “please finalize” prompt and accepts whatever comes back, even if it is incomplete and not prefixed with `Final Answer:`; no min length, no structure validation, no retry. See [`report_agent.py:1502`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py) through [`report_agent.py:1530`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py).

Second cause: context bloat gets extreme as the report progresses. Each section inherits up to 4000 characters from every previous section, with no global cap, and those are concatenated into the next prompt. See [`report_agent.py:1262`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py) through [`report_agent.py:1269`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py), and the accumulation loop in [`report_agent.py:1633`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py) through [`report_agent.py:1670`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py). On top of that, tool observations are injected verbatim, and some tools deliberately return full, untruncated dumps: `InsightForge` emits all facts/entities/chains, `Panorama` emits all active/historical facts and entities, and `InterviewResult` emits full interview transcripts. See [`zep_tools.py:170`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py) through [`zep_tools.py:210`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py), [`zep_tools.py:249`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py) through [`zep_tools.py:280`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py), and [`zep_tools.py:374`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py) through [`zep_tools.py:397`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py). That makes later sections especially likely to degrade into shallow completions.

I do not think this is a frontend truncation issue. The API returns full section content from disk with no shortening in [`report.py:630`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py) through [`report.py:645`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py), and the report view renders the whole stored section body in [`Step4Report.vue:49`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue) through [`Step4Report.vue:52`](file:///var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_6xahuttz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue). So if you see a stub there, it was almost certainly persisted that way.

If you want, I can patch this in the obvious places: add section-quality validation before accepting final text, summarize/tool-cap observations before reinjecting them, and cap prior-section context globally instead of `4000 * N`.

results.prompt:
Users keep asking why the final report can't include agent interviews after
the simulation is already over. We do have an interview_agents tool, so
everyone assumes this should work, but in practice it seems flaky or maybe
impossible. I can't tell if this is actually broken or just designed in a way
that makes the request invalid. Can you look into what is happening when
report generation uses agent interviews after a sim has ended?
bash_commands: codeir search report generation interview_agents simulation report
rg -n "class InterviewResult|def to_text\(self\).*Interview|summary = f\"采访失败|采访API调用失败|未找到可采访" backend/app/services/zep_tools.py
rg -n "采访失败|采访API调用失败|模拟环境未运行或已关闭|无采访记录|interview_agents" backend/uploads -g 'agent_log.jsonl' -g 'simulation.log' -g 'run_state.json' -g 'env_status.json'
codeir_commands: codeir search report generation interview_agents simulation report

codeir_invocations.command: codeir search report generation interview_agents simulation report
codeir_invocations.subject: report generation interview_agents simulation report

results.final_response:
What’s happening is a state mismatch, not a purely broken tool.

`interview_agents` is only valid while the live OASIS env is still alive. That requirement is explicit in the tool description and implementation in [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L522) and [zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L1272). The actual batch interview call hard-checks `env_status.json` and raises if the env is not alive in [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L1513).

The confusing part is that a simulation can be “completed” and still be interviewable. The runner marks status `COMPLETED` as soon as it sees `simulation_end` events in [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L617), but the parallel sim script then stays in “wait for commands” mode by default and keeps the env alive for interviews in [run_parallel_simulation.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py#L1516) and [run_parallel_simulation.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py#L1595). So “simulation ended” is not the real boundary. “Env closed” is.

Why it feels flaky during report generation:
- Report generation never checks whether interviews are still possible before starting in [report.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py#L24).
- The report agent always exposes `interview_agents` as an available tool in [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L945).
- The prompt nudges the model to mix tools and even hints at unused tools, including `interview_agents`, in [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L705) and [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1289).
- When interviews are unavailable, `zep_tools.interview_agents` converts that into a soft text result instead of a hard failure in [zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_0xn9kckz/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L1462). So the report continues, but with “采访失败…” text as if it were normal tool output.

Net: post-simulation interviews are not impossible by design, but they are only valid while the env remains open. Once the env has been closed, the request is invalid. The current report path does not model that distinction, so users see nondeterministic behavior depending on whether the env is still alive when the LLM decides to call `interview_agents`.

If you want, I can patch this so report generation either:
1. hides `interview_agents` when `env_alive` is false, or
2. fails fast with a clear message instead of letting the agent discover it mid-run.

results.prompt:
I'm wondering if our report agent is being too slow and over-ceremonial. It
feels like everything happens in this really plodding way. Why can't more
happen at once? What are we waiting for? If we changed this to make more
happen at the same time, what would that break or change in this codebase?
bash_commands: codeir grep "def insight_forge|def panorama_search|def quick_search|def interview_agents" --path backend/app/services/zep_tools.py -C 3
nl -ba backend/app/api/report.py | sed -n '100,170p'; nl -ba backend/app/api/report.py | sed -n '565,645p'
nl -ba frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue | sed -n '1715,1760p'; nl -ba frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue | sed -n '2028,2052p'
nl -ba backend/app/services/zep_tools.py | sed -n '945,1098p'; nl -ba backend/app/services/zep_tools.py | sed -n '1272,1398p'
nl -ba backend/app/services/report_agent.py | sed -n '35,110p'; nl -ba backend/app/services/report_agent.py | sed -n '306,380p'; nl -ba backend/app/services/report_agent.py | sed -n '2210,2260p'
codeir_commands: codeir grep "def insight_forge|def panorama_search|def quick_search|def interview_agents" --path backend/app/services/zep_tools.py -C 3

codeir_invocations.command: codeir grep "def insight_forge|def panorama_search|def quick_search|def interview_agents" --path backend/app/services/zep_tools.py -C 3
codeir_invocations.subject: def insight_forge|def panorama_search|def quick_search|def interview_agents

results.final_response:
Short answer: yes, the report agent is intentionally serialized in several places, and only some of that is necessary.

What is already parallel:
- The HTTP endpoint returns immediately and runs report generation in a background thread, so report work is already off the request path: [report.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py#L124).
- The `interview_agents` tool already batches interviews and, in dual-platform mode, asks both platforms at once via the simulation IPC batch API: [zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L1365), [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L1487).

What is making it feel plodding:
- Outline planning is a hard gate before any section work starts: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1598).
- Sections are generated in a strict `for` loop, one at a time: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1636).
- Each section is given all prior section content as context, so later sections explicitly depend on earlier ones: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1262), [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1655).
- Inside a section, the ReAct loop waits on one LLM call, then executes only the first tool call even if the model asked for several, then feeds that observation back into the next LLM turn: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1294), [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1418).
- The code also forces at least 3 tool uses before accepting a final answer, which adds ceremony even when the section may already have enough evidence: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1285), [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1375).

If you parallelize more, what changes or breaks:
- Section coherence changes first. Right now later sections can reference earlier ones; parallel section generation removes that guarantee unless you split sections into “independent” and “depends-on-prior” groups.
- Progress semantics break. The backend exposes one `current_section` and one linear `completed_sections` list: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L2215), [report.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py#L565). The frontend also assumes a single active section: [Step4Report.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue#L1748), [Step4Report.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue#L2036).
- Logging and file writes become racy. `agent_log.jsonl` appends and `progress.json` overwrites have no explicit locking: [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L96), [report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L2224).
- Load on dependencies rises sharply. `insight_forge` itself is serial and heavy, doing subquery search loops plus per-entity detail fetches: [zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L981), [zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_ktvxk2m6/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L1041). Running several sections at once multiplies Zep and LLM pressure.
- Output order changes. Section files are indexed, so final assembly can still be ordered, but logs and “latest completed section” UI behavior will arrive out of order.

The safest speedup in this codebase would be:
- Keep planning serial.
- Generate only independent sections in parallel.
- Keep each section’s ReAct loop serial, but allow a bounded worker pool across sections, like 2-3 concurrent sections.
- Replace single `current_section` with `active_sections`.
- Add locks or a queue around progress/log writes.

If you want, I can turn this into a concrete change plan and point out the exact files to edit first.

results.prompt:
We want to add one more report-generation tool that calls an external
analytics API. Would that be complicated? Before we wire it in, can you look
through this codebase and tell me what would actually have to change?
results.final_response:
It would be a moderate change, not a deep refactor. The report stack already supports external tool-style calls through the agent, and `interview_agents` is the existing precedent for that pattern in [backend/app/services/zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py#L1272). So if your new analytics API is “agent calls tool, gets text back, cites it in the report,” this is mostly additive.

The mandatory backend changes are all in the report agent/tool layer:
- Add the actual API wrapper method and result formatting in [backend/app/services/zep_tools.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py). That is where the current report tools live, and where the external interview flow is implemented.
- Register the tool in `ReportAgent._define_tools` in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L918).
- Add execution logic in `ReportAgent._execute_tool` in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L955).
- Add the tool name to the parser allowlist in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1064), or the model can emit the call and the agent will reject it.
- Update the prompt text and tool guidance in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L614) and the “mix different tools” list around [report_agent.py:705](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L705).
- Update the internal `all_tools` set used for “unused tool” nudges in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1289).

Things you probably do not need to change:
- The report generation API route in [backend/app/api/report.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py#L24). It just instantiates `ReportAgent` and runs generation.
- The basic report UI flow. The log viewer is generic enough to show unknown tool names.

Optional but likely useful changes:
- Add config/env vars for the new analytics endpoint/key in [backend/app/config.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/config.py#L20).
- Add nicer frontend labels/icons in [frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue#L495), and a structured renderer if the tool returns rich data instead of plain text around [Step4Report.vue:263](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue#L263).
- Improve chat “sources” output in [backend/app/services/report_agent.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_z5xc8dqs/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py#L1840), because right now it only extracts `parameters.query`; a tool that uses different params would show weak source metadata.

The main hidden coupling is that tool support is duplicated in several places inside `report_agent.py`, not centralized. So the work is straightforward, but it is easy to miss one of the prompt/allowlist/guidance sets and end up with a tool that exists in code but is never selected by the model.

One more practical point: there do not appear to be dedicated tests around this tool plumbing, so I would expect to add a small integration-style test or at least a mocked execution check when wiring it in.

If you want, I can turn this into a concrete implementation checklist for “add analytics API tool X” with exact file edits and a recommended shape for the response object.

results.prompt:
When a simulation finishes and I search report features, everything from the
last session isn't there or is partly there. I have to wait for it to have
everything. What's going on there?
bash_commands: codeir search "report agent search quick_search panorama_search" --category core_logic
codeir search "SimulationRunner report_agent zep_tools quick_search panorama_search"
rg -n "def search_graph|def get_all_nodes|def get_all_edges|class ZepToolsService" backend/app/services/zep_tools.py -n -S
rg -n "generateReport|handleNextStep|runner_status|COMPLETED|Next Step|进入.*报告|报告生成" frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue -S
codeir_commands: codeir search "report agent search quick_search panorama_search" --category core_logic
codeir search "SimulationRunner report_agent zep_tools quick_search panorama_search"

codeir_invocations.command: codeir search "report agent search quick_search panorama_search" --category core_logic
codeir_invocations.subject: report agent search quick_search panorama_search

codeir_invocations.command: codeir search "SimulationRunner report_agent zep_tools quick_search panorama_search"
codeir_invocations.subject: SimulationRunner report_agent zep_tools quick_search panorama_search

results.final_response:
What was happening: the app treated the simulation as “finished” before the report search data was actually finished syncing into the graph.

The concrete race was in [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_5jugp9o7/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L536): the run could be marked completed as soon as the process exited or `simulation_end` was seen, but the Zep graph updater was still flushing batched activities afterward. Report search uses the graph directly, so `quick_search` / `panorama_search` could see only part of the latest session until that async sync caught up.

I changed it so completion now means “process finished and graph sync finished,” exposed that readiness in run-state, gated the Step 3 UI on it in [Step3Simulation.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_5jugp9o7/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue#L512), and made report generation reject early starts while sync is still pending in [report.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_5jugp9o7/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py#L73).

Verification: `python -m py_compile backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py backend/app/api/report.py` passed. I did not run a frontend build in this turn.
